claims.claim: Under this Master Services Agreement, 'Radio Equipment' is defined to include the Antenna Systems, cables between the Antenna System and System Technology, cables between System Technology and line termination, and any distributors such as RF couplers and RF diplexers, but expressly excludes the System Technology itself.
The agreement provides that a Change of Control of TowerCo includes any person directly or indirectly owning shares in TowerCo representing at least 33.3% of the voting stock.
Either party may terminate the agreement at the expiration of the Initial Term by giving at least one hundred and eighty (180) days’ prior written notice.

Under the Agreement, SaaS Fees during the Initial Term may increase by a cumulative maximum of fifteen percent (15%) above the original fees over the life of the five-year Initial Term, calculated as three annual increases of up to CPI plus two percent (2%) each beginning after the second year.
Section 3.2 effectively bars Kealey from using any of his Prior Inventions in his work for the Company unless and until the Company gives prior written consent.
The MOU requires Buyer to enter into any Monetizer Coal Supply Agreement or Third Party Coal Supply Agreement proposed by Seller after execution of the MOU.

Under Section –, the contract prohibits DNA from selling or distributing any Authorized Products to a Customer without first obtaining a written agreement from the Customer that includes the Standard Resale Terms, but it explicitly allows DNA to sell Authorized Products to potential Customers without such an agreement.

Following the Manufacturing Agreement Termination Effective Date, Bacthera is required to support the transfer of the Manufacturing Process to NESA or its designee, including site visits to the Facility and coverage of any associated transfer support costs.

Under Section 1(a), the license is automatically terminated if the Distribution and Supply Agreement becomes effective on a date after the License Commencement Date, ensuring the two agreements never coexist.

Section 3(B) requires Licensor to file and diligently prosecute trademark applications in any jurisdiction where Licensee demonstrates a legitimate business need for such registration, unless doing so could materially and adversely affect the Trademark in that jurisdiction.
The agreement provides that DMC will remove the restrictions on the 39,083 unvested restricted shares upon receipt of Employee’s signed Severance Agreement.

Paragraph 5 requires the undersigned to reimburse all sums expended by the Lender within five days after written demand, and those sums automatically bear interest at the default rate set forth in the Note from the date of the Lender’s expenditure until paid.

Under clause 7.2.3, when a Receiving Party is required by law or a regulatory authority to disclose Confidential Information, it must first give notice to the Disclosing Party before making any such disclosure.

Under the Amendment, Franchisee is obligated to pay a combined total of $70,329.92 to Franchisor, which covers both the past due amounts owed on the Hotel and Franchisor's outside legal counsel fees and expenses incurred in connection with the Amendment.
In connection with Franchisor’s consent to the Equity Contribution, the Amendment requires Franchisee to pay Marriott a transfer fee concurrent with the Effective Date.

Section 2 entitles one or more Holders that collectively own at least 50% of the outstanding Registrable Securities to require the Corporation to file a registration statement covering at least 20% of those securities at any time after the closing of the Corporation’s first public offering.

Under Section 2 of the Agreement, a registration demand may only be initiated by Holders who collectively hold at least fifty percent (50%) of the then outstanding Registrable Securities, and such demand must specify that the registration will cover Registrable Securities with an expected public offering price of at least $10,000,000, both conditions being required simultaneously for the Corporation to be obligated to file a registration statement.

Section 2 effectively gives Silicon Valley Bank no registration rights under the agreement, because the demand registration provision excludes Silicon Valley Bank and its permitted transferees from making a written request.

Under Section 2(b), Ragan and Lawson agree to vote all of their beneficially owned Anchor shares in accordance with the Board's recommendation on any proposal brought before Anchor shareholders during the Standstill Period, including proposals submitted by the Board itself.

Under the Loan Agreement, the Interbank Rate is determined by reference to the rate quoted for dollar deposits in the interbank Eurodollar market at approximately 9:00 A.M. Milwaukee time on the first day of each calendar month, and M&I is required to use the Knight Ridder Information Service to obtain such rate information.
Section 2.2.1 provides that SIIPL and SLS may reject any Proposed Purchase Order unless it exactly matches the quantities and delivery dates set forth in the applicable SOW.

The LOI provides that, effective upon execution, Vivendi has the right to deduct from any and all Interplay Proceeds owed under the New Distribution Agreement any amounts owed by Interplay to Vivendi under the Old Distribution Agreement for General Reserve true-ups.
Section 5 entitles Consultant to reimbursement for all reasonable travel and other expenses incurred in connection with services under the agreement, so long as he provides appropriate documentation.

Reading Sections 1(d) and 2(a) together, IMPAX is granted the right to sublicense not only DAVA but also any other distributor acting in a similar capacity to DAVA, provided such distributor's sales and shipments of IMPAX Product to bona fide purchasers occur prior to the License Termination Date.

Under Section 6.3(b), a breaching party has a combined maximum cure period of 60 days—comprising the initial 30-day cure window plus an additional 30-day extension that the Parties may mutually agree upon—before the non-defaulting Party may terminate the agreement.

The agreement defines 'Change in Control' to include the sale of substantially all of Middlefield's assets, and explicitly states that the sale of the assets of The Middlefield Banking Company alone does not qualify as such a sale.

Under Section 2.1, the defined term 'Collateral' expressly includes RV Adjustment Funds whenever they are deposited into the Lease Funding Account, the Company Account, or any Exchange Note Account in accordance with the Master Exchange Agreement.
The Regulations provide that the Lessee may early redeem the Leasing Subject by paying the Amount of Early Performance of Obligations stated in the relevant Appendix.

Under the Second Amendment's Applicable Rate table for Revolving Loans, the combined Eurocurrency Rate and Commitment Fee at Pricing Level I totals 3.80% per annum, representing the highest aggregate rate tier applicable when the Total Leverage Ratio equals or exceeds 4.00x.
The sum of all commitment fees across the six pricing levels for Revolving Loans, as defined in the amended Applicable Rate schedule, totals 2.60% per annum.
VERIFIED CLAUSE: The contract requires KV to pay the royalty within 45 days after each calendar quarter, but no later than 75 days after the License Termination Date for any outstanding Net Sales.

The aggregate principal amount of all promissory notes issued by AXS-One Inc. under the Security Agreement (as amended through October 30, 2008) totals Twelve Million Nine Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars ($12,950,000), representing the combined amounts of the May 2007 Notes, November 2007 Notes, July 2008 Notes, and October 2008 Notes.
The total aggregate principal amount of all notes secured by the Security Agreement as amended by this Third Amendment is $11,950,000.

VERIFIED CLAUSE: Under clause 7.1, the Receiving Party is permitted to disclose Confidential Information to third parties without prior written consent whenever such disclosure is reasonably necessary for the performance of the Services.

If Cantor still holds the 200,000 Specified Shares on the Acquisition Closing Date, the Amendment provides that the Common Stock portion of the Deferred Underwriting Commission will be reduced by $2,000,000 and the cash portion paid from the Trust Account will be increased by the same $2,000,000 amount.

The Escrow Agreement provides that ATI may instruct the Escrow Agent to release the full $6,000,000 of Escrow Property to ATI once the Tender Offer Conditions are satisfied or waived by the Expiration Date and ATI agrees to accept the Shares for purchase.
VERIFIED CLAUSE: The Maintenance Period for Hosted Applications is defined as 08:30 - 17:30, Client's head office time zone, on all days excluding weekends and NSB Local Holidays.

Section 3.1 effectively guarantees Boot Barn a minimum three-year commitment term before either party can end the SaaS relationship without cause, since Client’s first contractual early exit right does not arise until after the end of the third year of the Initial Term.

The aggregate principal amount of all promissory notes issued by AXS-One Inc. across the May 2007, November 2007, July 2008, and October 2008 financings totals Eleven Million Nine Hundred and Fifty Thousand Dollars ($11,950,000), as reflected in the cumulative secured obligations under the Security Agreement.
The Amendment stipulates that the total Deferred Underwriting Commission payable to Cantor, combining cash and stock, is capped at $7,280,000 regardless of Third Party Equity Value.
Section 5.2 allows either Party to disclose this Trademark License Agreement whenever required by applicable law or stock exchange rules, without needing the other Party’s consent.

The aggregate principal amount of all promissory notes issued by AXS-One Inc. under the May 2007, November 2007, July 2008, and October 2008 financings combined totals Eleven Million Nine Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars ($11,950,000), as reflected across the recitals of this Amendment.

Under the amended rate table, the maximum combined Applicable Margin for A Term Loans maintained as Eurodollar Loans plus the Applicable Commitment Commission Percentage at the highest leverage level (Level 5) totals 2.750%.

Because Preamble (G) says TowerCo may, in its entire discretion, execute all activities it deems necessary for optimisation and deployment of the TowerCo Infrastructure Network, the agreement expressly gives TowerCo sole decision-making authority over network optimisation and deployment without requiring Sunrise’s prior approval.
The Assumption Agreement adds 60 software license agreements to Schedule 6, representing the total number of licenses TheNextRound, Inc. has granted as of the agreement date.

Under the Regulations, the Tariffs are expressly stated to be an integral part of the Lease Agreement itself, meaning any unilateral changes to the Tariffs by the Lessor automatically become binding terms of each individual Lease Agreement without requiring the Lessee's separate consent.

Under the Amended Credit Agreement, the defined term 'Borrowers' includes Columbus McKinnon Corporation, Columbus McKinnon EMEA GmbH, and all Subsidiaries of the Company that are party to the Credit Agreement, because the parenthetical definition in the Amendment's preamble expressly groups 'the Company and any other Designated Borrower' together with those Subsidiaries.

VERIFIED CLAUSE: The Amendment certifies that, as of the Second Amendment Effective Date, the Company's representations and warranties under Section 5.21 of the Credit Agreement are true and correct in all respects without any materiality qualification.

Under the combined effect of Section 1(g) and Section 1(h), if a force majeure event excuses a third party supplier from delivering coal under a Monetizer Coal Supply Agreement, Seller is nonetheless required to step in and perform those deliveries on the third party supplier's behalf, since a force majeure event under the Agreement is deemed a force majeure event under the Monetizer Coal Supply Agreement but not vice versa.

Under Section 5.2, each purchase order submitted by DNA must include at least five categories of minimum information, including the name and description of the potential Customer, intended end-use of the Authorized Products, country of ultimate destination, credit information if requested, and a copy of the executed or proposed Customer Agreement.

Because Section 3 states Dyer is entitled to a $25,000 Consulting Fee each calendar quarter commencing with the Date of Termination and Section 14 says the agreement may be canceled only by mutual written agreement, Company cannot unilaterally stop paying quarterly fees before the first anniversary absent Dyer’s written consent.

The Manufacturing Agreement Termination Agreement identifies a total of fourteen (14) specific sections of the Manufacturing Agreement that survive termination and remain in full force and effect after the Manufacturing Agreement Termination Effective Date.

If the APA closing does not occur by December 31, 2024 or the APA is terminated earlier, this Assignment and Termination Agreement automatically terminates and the Manufacturing Agreement remains in full force and effect between Seres and Bacthera as though no assignment or suspension had ever occurred.

Because Section 7(f) preserves Employee’s right to participate in government investigations while Section 8 broadly bars disclosure of the agreement’s terms, the Severance Agreement specifically permits Employee to disclose the agreement and its consideration to any federal, state, or local government agency without Employer’s consent.
The Agreement provides that DMC shall remove the restrictions on Employee’s 39,083 unvested restricted shares upon receipt of the signed Severance Agreement.
Under the amended clause 3.23, a Material Adverse Effect that occurs after November 5, 2020 but before the Completion Date on November 27, 2020 would be a condition that prevents Completion.
Under Section 7.1, if a Jeopardy Event occurs, both Purchaser and Seller have the right to immediately cancel the Agreement if an amendment cannot be negotiated within 30 days.

Because clause 8.1 excludes third-party rights and clause 9.1 states that nothing in the agreement creates an agent/principal relationship, neither Party has authority to bind the other in any contracts with third parties made in connection with the Joint Activities.

VERIFIED CLAUSE: Clause 3.3 establishes that the Joint Venture Partner's performance is the sole consideration for Mastek's obligations, creating a mutual dependency where neither party may invoice the other for Services.

The definition of 'Good Industry Practice' in clause 1.1 requires compliance with applicable legislation and the standard of care expected from a skilled operator engaged in the business of providing services which are the same as or similar to the 'Joint Activities'.

Clause 9.1 makes clear that, despite the agreement being styled a joint venture, neither Party has authority to bind the other in contracts with third parties or incur liabilities on the other’s behalf.
Clause 7.2.3 requires the Receiving Party to give prior written notice to the other Party before making any legally compelled disclosure of Confidential Information.

Under the Amendment, the defined term 'Owner' is updated to refer to Moody National 1 Polito Lyndhurst Holding, LLC in place of both Moody National CY Lyndhurst S, LLC and Moody National CY Lyndhurst H, LLC, and this redefinition applies retroactively to all provisions of the Franchise Agreement including those predating the original August 31, 2007 agreement date.
Under the Amendment, the defined term 'Owner' now refers to Moody National 1 Polito Lyndhurst Holding, LLC for all purposes in the Franchise Agreement, including the QA Program obligations.
As a condition of Marriott’s consent to the Equity Contribution, the Amendment requires Franchisee to pay Marriott a transfer fee concurrent with the Effective Date.

Under the Agreement, the 'Execution Date of the Settlement Agreement' is defined as the date upon which all Parties and counsel listed as signatories have executed it, and this date serves as the trigger for when the Court may set alternative dates or deadlines in place of those designated on Exhibit A for defined terms such as the Closing Date and Fairness Hearing Date.

Because Section 2(b) requires Ragan and Lawson to vote all of their Anchor shares in line with the Board’s recommendation on any shareholder proposal during the Standstill Period, they are effectively barred from publicly opposing or campaigning against such proposals.
Under Section 2, the total combined beneficial ownership of Anchor common stock that Lawson, Ragan, and all Lawson Affiliates are permitted to acquire during the Standstill Period is capped at 9.99%.

If Licensor declares bankruptcy, becomes insolvent, or ceases its software business, the Licensee will immediately receive the Source Code and Commentary with an unlimited right to use and modify them without restriction.

Because the Substitution was made effective as of December 15, 2009 and Schedule I of the Mortgage was replaced in its entirety with Schedule A, all Removed Aircraft ceased to be subject to the Security Agreement as of that date and were automatically released from the collateral pool.

Under the Amendment, the term 'Borrowers' is defined to include International Lease Finance Corporation, States Aircraft, Inc., Shrewsbury Aircraft Leasing Limited, and Top Aircraft, Inc., collectively obligating all four entities as co-borrowers under both Credit Agreements.

The Amendment provides that the Parent Borrower shall remove certain Pool Aircraft from the Designated Pool and provide Non-Pool Aircraft of reasonably equivalent appraised value in substitution for the Removed Aircraft.

The Amendment replaces a total of nine schedules across the Credit Agreements and the Mortgage, with five schedules amended in the Credit Agreement and four schedules amended in the Amended and Restated Credit Agreement.

The amendment makes the Substitution effective as of December 15, 2009 and, by replacing Schedule I of the Mortgage with Schedule A, it confirms that every aircraft listed on Schedule A automatically became part of the Designated Pool on that date as substitute collateral for the Removed Aircraft.
The attached Schedule A lists a total of 51 aircraft as collateral under the amended Aircraft Mortgage and Security Agreement.
The agreement defines an Event of Default to include any final judgment entered against the Company or any Subsidiary in excess of $5,000,000.
The agreement provides that M&I will determine LIBOR using the Knight Ridder Information Service at 9:00 A.M. Milwaukee time on the first Business Day of each month.
The LOI grants Vivendi an immediate right of offset against any amounts owed to Interplay under the New Distribution Agreement.

The LOI establishes a combined total of three separate amendment obligations across the Old Distribution Agreement and the New Distribution Agreement, specifically: (1) removing *** from the Old Distribution Agreement, (2) adding *** to the New Distribution Agreement as a Partner Product on Exhibit B, and (3) reducing the General Reserve true-up settlement period under Section 6.8(b) from the original timeframe to a shortened period, resulting in an aggregate of three distinct contractual modifications that together constitute the complete scope of amendments contemplated by the LOI.

The Right of Offset amendment applies to any and all amounts owed from Interplay to Vivendi under the Old Distribution Agreement, including but not limited to those arising from general reserve true-ups.
The LOI obligates the parties to execute the Definitive Agreement within a reasonable time after signing the New Distribution Agreement, using good faith efforts to finalize the detailed amendments.

Under Section 6, 'Confidential Information' expressly includes any areas of inquiry by the Special Committee of the Board of Directors regardless of whether such information is already in the public domain, thereby carving out Special Committee inquiry topics from the general public-domain exclusion that otherwise applies to all other categories of Confidential Information.
Section 8 provides that upon termination of the Second Consulting Agreement, Consultant will be paid any unpaid compensation for services rendered during the 10-day notice period.

Under the combined terms of Sections 2 and 8, the maximum total notice-period compensation Consultant could receive upon termination is US$3,500, calculated as the 10-day notice period multiplied by the US$350 hourly rate.
ELITE shall be responsible for any customer bill back penalties incurred by TPN whenever a shipment is delayed more than five business days beyond the delivery date.
Section 2.4 makes TPN financially committed to purchase the full quantities listed in the first three months of each 12-month Forecast, because that Firm Period is binding on TPN.

Under the Agreement's definition of 'Sold' in Section 1(a), shipments of IMPAX Product from IMPAX to DAVA for DAVA's subsequent resale to third parties count toward calculating the License Amount, because the definition expressly covers sales and shipments by 'any party acting on their respective behalves' to a third party bona fide purchaser for commercial sale.

Because Section 2.5 keeps the security interest in force until the later of payment in full of all Secured Obligations and termination of all Commitments, and Section 2.4 authorizes the Closed-End Collateral Agent to file continuation statements as necessary, the agreement requires the Collateral Agent to maintain perfected UCC filings until the Final Release Date.

Section 2.1 states that the Collateral includes all Closed-End Collections on the Closed-End Units, and the definition of Closed-End Collections, as incorporated from Appendix A of the Collateral Agency Agreement, encompasses all payments received by the Borrower from any source related to the Closed-End Units, including residual value guarantees.
The Agreement allows either party to terminate at the expiration of the Initial Term by giving the other party at least 180 days’ prior written notice.

Reading Section 3.1 and Section 3.3 together, if Client terminates for NSB's material breach after the third year of the Initial Term, Client is expressly relieved of the fifty percent (50%) early-termination fee that would otherwise apply, because the termination is attributable to NSB's default rather than Client's convenience.
The net profit bonus is payable annually on the same date each year, specifically upon completion of the audit and filing of the Form 10-K.
VERIFIED CLAUSE: The Employee's vehicle allowance of $1,100 per month is payable on the first day of each month, retroactive to January 1, 2013.

Under the naked-license doctrine reflected in UCC § 2-306’s good-faith standards, Section 4(A) requires Licensor to respond to any proposed new trademark prototype within fifteen (15) calendar days or the use is automatically deemed approved.

Section 3(B) effectively requires the Licensee to bear all costs of trademark protection in the Territory, including the expense of maintaining and renewing existing trademark registrations used in the Container Vessel Business.
Section 2 states that, to the best of the undersigned’s knowledge, no Hazardous Material is currently located at, on, in, under or about the Trust Property.

Under paragraph 5, all sums expended by the Lender must be reimbursed within five (5) days after written demand, and any unreimbursed amounts bear interest at the default interest rate set forth in the Note, creating a combined reimbursement obligation covering both paragraphs 4 and 5 indemnities.

Reading paragraphs 5 and 6 together, the agreement provides that any sums expended by the Lender in connection with enforcement of the Guaranty and Indemnification Agreement that are not reimbursed within five days of written demand are automatically charged against the undersigned's deposit accounts and securities held by the Lender without any further notice or demand, and such set-off amounts shall themselves bear interest at the default rate specified in the Note.

The amended definition of 'Notes' in Section 2(b) expressly includes the October 2008 Notes within the scope of the original Security Agreement dated May 29, 2007, thereby making the October 2008 Notes subject to all of the same covenants, representations, and warranties that applied to the May 2007 Notes at the time of their original issuance.

The aggregate principal amount of all promissory notes issued by AXS-One Inc. across the May 2007, November 2007, July 2008, and October 2008 financings totals Eleven Million Nine Hundred and Fifty Thousand Dollars ($11,950,000), representing the total secured obligations under the Security Agreement as amended.

Section 5 of the Amendment expressly provides that the October 2008 Notes rank pari passu with the May 2007 Notes, November 2007 Notes, and July 2008 Notes in priority of payment and in all other respects, and that this pari passu ranking applies unconditionally regardless of the order in which the respective notes were issued or the security interests were granted.
The total principal amount secured by this Amendment across all notes (May 2007, November 2007, July 2008, and October 2008) is $12,850,000.

Under the Amendment's definition of 'Third Party Private Investors,' any investment funds affiliated with or managed by Cantor Fitzgerald & Co. are expressly excluded from that term, meaning proceeds from Cantor-affiliated equity investments would not count toward the Third Party Equity Value calculation.

Under this Agreement, the Corporation's combined deferral and postponement rights under Section 2 allow it to delay a requested registration for a total maximum period of up to six (6) months in any twelve-month period, calculated by adding the three-month good-faith deferral period under clause (b) and the three-month audit postponement period under clause (c).

Because Section 2 allows a written demand only after six months following the Corporation’s first public offering, the Agreement does not grant any piggyback registration rights before the IPO and all registration rights become effective only after that six-month post-IPO period.
ATI may instruct the Escrow Agent to disburse the full $6,000,000 of Escrow Property to ATI once the Tender Offer Conditions are satisfied or waived and ATI agrees to accept the Shares for purchase.

The amendment provides that the up to $20,000,000 Restricted Payment to the Sponsors to fund Acquisition III LLC’s purchase of the Managing GP may be treated as an Investment permitted under Section 6.7(r).

The Amendment expressly requires that the net proceeds from the sale of the Managing GP to Acquisition III LLC must be applied in accordance with Section 2.14(a) AND that such sale price must equal at least $20,000,000, because the GP Purchase Price is defined as the amount of the Restricted Payment actually paid to the Sponsors, which Section 6.5(a)(x) caps at $20,000,000.

The Amendment requires that the Borrowers deliver a signed counterpart of the Amendment to the Administrative Agent via telecopier no later than the date on which the Second Amendment is dated, July 15, 2004, for the Amendment to become effective.

Because the license is exclusive in the Territory and Field and remains in effect until the earlier of termination of the JV Contract or Licensor’s exercise of the put option, Licensor is prohibited during that entire period from using or licensing the Licensed Trademarks to any other party in the PRC, Hong Kong, Macau, or Taiwan for Licensed Products.
